mnemonics.title: OS FUNCTIONS REMEMBER
mnemonics.content: TO REMEMBER BASIC OS FUNCTIONS USE: EAT IDFER (EXECUTION, ACCOUNTING, TERMINATION, I/O, DEBUGGING, FILE MANAGEMENT, ERROR DETECTION, RESOURCE ALLOCATION)

mnemonics.title: TYPES OF OS
mnemonics.content: TO REMEMBER OS TYPES: B T R D (BATCH, TIME-SHARING, REAL-TIME, DISTRIBUTED)

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM COMPONENTS
mnemonics.content: TO REMEMBER MAIN OS COMPONENTS: P M F D S N (PROCESS, MEMORY, FILE, DEVICE, SECURITY, NETWORK)

mnemonics.title: BOOT PROCESS
mnemonics.content: TO REMEMBER BOOT STEPS: P S R K (POWER ON, SELF-TEST, READ BOOTSTRAP, KERNEL LOAD)

mnemonics.title: KERNEL MODE SERVICES
mnemonics.content: TO REMEMBER KERNEL SERVICES: P S M F P (PROCESS SCHEDULING, MEMORY MANAGEMENT, FILE I/O, PROCESS CREATION, PROTECTION)

acronyms.usage: The fundamental software that manages computer hardware and provides services to applications
Set of functions and procedures that allow applications to use OS services
Data structure maintained by OS to store information about each process
The primary hardware component that executes program instructions
Operations involving data transfer between CPU and external devices
Primary memory where OS and programs are loaded for execution
Visual interface allowing user interaction through icons and windows

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A CAKE LAYER DIAGRAM: TOP = USER APPLICATIONS, MIDDLE = SYSTEM PROGRAMS AND SHELL, BOTTOM = KERNEL, BASE = HARDWARE. EACH LAYER USES SERVICES FROM LAYER BELOW IT.

visualTips.visualize: ARROW FLOW: USER PROGRAM → LIBRARY WRAPPER → SYSTEM CALL INTERFACE → KERNEL → HARDWARE → RETURN TO USER PROGRAM
DRAW MEMORY AS RECTANGLE DIVIDED INTO BLOCKS: ONE BLOCK FOR OS, MULTIPLE BLOCKS FOR USER PROCESSES (SOME WAITING, SOME RUNNING)
SEQUENCE DIAGRAM: POWER BUTTON → BIOS/UEFI → POST → BOOT LOADER → OS KERNEL → LOGIN SCREEN

analogies.analogy: JUST AS A GOVERNMENT MANAGES RESOURCES LIKE ROADS, SCHOOLS, AND HOSPITALS FOR CITIZENS, THE OS MANAGES CPU TIME, MEMORY, AND FILES FOR PROGRAMS.
LIKE THE CENTRAL GOVERNMENT MAKES CRITICAL DECISIONS FOR THE COUNTRY, THE KERNEL MAKES CRITICAL DECISIONS FOR THE COMPUTER SYSTEM.
JUST AS CITIZENS FILL REQUEST FORMS TO ACCESS GOVERNMENT SERVICES, PROGRAMS USE SYSTEM CALLS TO REQUEST OS SERVICES.
LIKE A RECEPTIONIST INTERPRETS VISITOR REQUESTS AND DIRECTS THEM TO APPROPRIATE DEPARTMENTS, THE SHELL INTERPRETS USER COMMANDS AND EXECUTES PROGRAMS.
LIKE A TRANSLATOR HELPS TWO PEOPLE SPEAKING DIFFERENT LANGUAGES UNDERSTAND EACH OTHER, DEVICE DRIVERS HELP OS UNDERSTAND HARDWARE.
